title: 1981-04-16_Pat_124.txt
key_claims: The old usurper King has cast a spell (Dokos/seeming) over the land, keeping everyone in a prison. The young true King (Jesus) knows this, but speaks in riddles and parables to trigger anamnesis only in the "elect" who can solve the riddle.
The dialogue between Jesus and Pilate is a ritual drama between the true King and the usurper's representative; both know the truth but neither states it outright.

Christ's Kingdom (Coaxial World B) and the Empire (Coaxial World A) occupy the same space-time but operate under completely different laws and information systems. Jesus was a "Wizard" performing white magic by utilizing the laws of World B while standing in World A.
Thomas (the first-century Christian) was not a reincarnated past life, but rather the internal analog of Coaxial World B. When World B invaded Dick's reality, Thomas invaded Dick's mind.
recurring_concepts: **The Dual Function of Objects**: Every object, act, or event exists simultaneously in both Coaxial World A and Coaxial World B, but possesses completely different (often opposite) significance and value in each.
reading_excerpt: The old usurper King has cast a spell (Dokos/seeming) over the land, keeping everyone in a prison. The young true King (Jesus) knows this, but speaks in riddles and parables to trigger anamnesis only in the "elect" who can solve the riddle.
